findings.message: D2 says trait method edges are added for `EdgeSet::Methods | EdgeSet::All`, but the rendering note relies on trait nodes already appearing via `EdgeSet::Impls`. That leaves method-only/default outputs without an explicit way to render trait sources, and the clustered pipeline classifies clusters from type nodes only, so trait-source edges are not guaranteed to appear. The plan needs to specify trait-node assignment/rendering independent of impl edges for D2 to be executable.

D3 requires `render_type_graph_overview` to collect parameter-edge indices and apply `linkStyle`, but the overview collapses multiple cross-cluster edges into one `(source_cluster, target_cluster)` count. A single overview edge can represent a mix of return-derived and parameter-derived edges, so there is no unambiguous parameter edge index to style unless the plan splits overview groups by origin/kind or excludes overview from this styling rule.

`T002` adds a distinct `EdgeSet::Methods`-without-`Impls` branch that explicitly inserts trait nodes into the cluster plan, but `T004` never schedules a test that exercises that edge case. The test list covers trait-method edges and linkStyle output, yet it does not verify the self-contained cluster behavior introduced here, so the plan does not actually cover all stated behavioral changes.

`render_type_graph_flat` is catalogued as modified only by D1 and D3, but the same description says its output includes `trait_method_param` / `trait_method_return`, which are introduced by D2. Since the spec defines D2 as a `collect_edges()` change for `EdgeSet::Methods | EdgeSet::All`, flat rendering is affected too; omitting D2/IN-04 from this entry makes the type catalogue inconsistent and can leave flat-mode trait-method coverage out of the implementation/test mapping.

T003 is infeasible as written: it says the old catch-all arm is replaced by explicit named arms while the plan still treats `edge_kind` as string values. A Rust dispatch over `&str` cannot be exhaustive with only literal arms, so following this plan would either fail to compile or require an unplanned fallback/type change.

`render_type_graph_flat` is internally inconsistent: its description says flat output will include `trait_method_param` / `trait_method_return`, but the entry attributes the function only to D1/D3 and its `spec_refs` omit IN-04, the spec requirement that introduces trait method scanning. Since `collect_edges()` is shared by flat and clustered rendering, D2 either affects flat rendering and must be catalogued/tested there, or the description should not claim those trait edge kinds appear in flat output.

`render_type_graph_flat` is described as also applying the EdgeSet::Methods-without-Impls trait-node cluster insertion and carries `IN-05` as a spec ref, but this entry is explicitly the non-clustered renderer and its signature has no `ClusterPlan`. That makes the catalogue assign a clustered-rendering concern to the flat renderer, conflicting with the spec’s cluster-only IN-05 behavior and potentially sending implementation work to the wrong surface.

`render_type_graph_flat` is explicitly catalogued as the flat/non-clustered renderer, but its description says the `EdgeSet::Methods`-without-`Impls` trait-node cluster insertion also applies and its `spec_refs` include `IN-05`. `IN-05` is a cluster-plan concern for inserting trait nodes into clustered output, so assigning it to the flat renderer is semantically wrong and can send implementation work to the wrong surface.

S4 says the new tests cover all behavioral changes from T001-T003, but the listed cases omit T002's distinct `EdgeSet::Methods`-without-`Impls` trait-node cluster insertion behavior. The plan therefore claims full behavioral test coverage while leaving one stated behavior without an explicit test case.

`render_type_graph_overview` is catalogued as `reference` with behavior stable across D1/D2/D3, but D1/D2 expand `collect_edges()` and overview builds its cross-cluster groups from those collected edges when needed. OS-03 only excludes D3 `linkStyle` coloring; it does not make overview edge content stable. This can misdirect implementation/tests to ignore overview count/group changes from receiver-less or trait-method edges. Mark overview as behavior-affected by D1/D2, or narrow the stability claim to signature/no linkStyle only.

`impl-plan.json` no longer records `commit_hash: null` for any task. The plan-artifact update is supposed to advance only T001's status while leaving task commit hashes unset until the finalize commit; deleting the key from T001-T004 changes the task schema/content beyond that status transition and removes the explicit pending-hash state the track workflow relies on.

`impl-plan.json` now records a `commit_hash` for T001, and the rendered plan exposes it, while the accepted tracking lifecycle for this T002 status-only update says task hashes remain unrecorded until the finalization commit. This creates a mixed state where T001 is hash-recorded but T002 is merely marked done, contradicting the stated plan-artifact update semantics.

The new `trait_method_*` branches in `collect_edges`, `render_type_graph_clustered`, and `render_type_graph_overview` do not have matching tests in this change set. Existing tests still cover only the pre-existing `method`/`impl` paths, so regressions in the new trait-method scan, trait-node insertion, or overview filtering can slip through unnoticed.

P1: `collect_edges()` drops valid trait-method edges when a trait and a workspace type share the same short name, because the new trait scan uses `target != trait_name.as_str()` as if this were a self-loop. That comparison is namespace-blind, so a trait `Foo` returning/accepting a type `Foo` is silently omitted even though the renderer already disambiguates the nodes with `_trait_foo`. This loses real edges in both flat and clustered diagrams.

The new trait-method scan emits edges without the same `target != trait_name` self-loop guard used for inherent methods. If a workspace trait and type share a short name, `collect_edges()` will now produce a spurious `_trait_Foo --> Foo` edge in both flat and clustered renders, which contradicts the intended D2 behavior and can pollute diagrams.

`render_type_graph_clustered` now short-circuits every `trait_method_*` edge before the existing cross-target path, so a trait that has both in-cluster and out-of-cluster method references only shows the in-cluster subset. The out-of-cluster dependencies disappear entirely once the trait node is inserted, which makes the cluster diagram incomplete for multi-cluster traits and diverges from the edge set collected in `collect_edges`.

The new `trait_method_*` paths in `collect_edges`, `render_type_graph_flat`, `render_type_graph_clustered`, and `render_type_graph_overview` are not covered by any regression tests here. Because this change also adds truncation and cluster-filtering logic around those branches, a regression could silently drop or misrender trait edges without being caught.

The new legend line is malformed in both renderers (`line 243` and again at `line 763`): Markdown will close the HTML comment immediately after `Edge legend:`, so the remainder of the string is emitted as visible text (`= return-derived, --o = argument-derived (gray) -->`) before the heading. That corrupts every generated flat/clustered file and defeats the intended hidden header comment.
